tasks.description: Build the authoritative migration inventory before editing code: grep the full panes surface (`PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, `usePanes()`), enumerate all panes-related tests/mocks, capture the repo-wide `createContext` declaration inventory with the generic-aware regex, and capture the repo-wide exported hook inventory (`export const/function use...`) so the later audit includes recomposed context-access hook families outside the original context/provider directories.

Implement `src/shared/state/panesStore.ts` as the new selector-first panes state surface, mirroring current pane behavior and provider-owned logic: preserve pane open/lock/dimension/effective-height/`activeTaskId` semantics, fold in the current pane lock policy bootstrap behavior, add an explicit singleton lifecycle contract including an idempotent bootstrap path and a test reset helper, and define the intended behavior for outside-bootstrap reads so tests can assert it intentionally.

Replace `PanesProvider` in the app provider tree with the panes-store bootstrap wiring while preserving mount order and initialization timing relative to `ProjectProvider`, `RealtimeProvider`, `IncomingTasksProvider`, and `AgentChatProvider`; then migrate every former panes consumer, including high-level panes readers, from `usePanes()` to dedicated selectors or explicitly narrow grouped selectors without recreating a broad compatibility facade.

Migrate panes-related tests and mocks to the store/bootstrap model, replace the old `PanesContext` test coverage with store/bootstrap-focused coverage, ensure singleton reset/lifecycle behavior is exercised, and then delete the old panes context surface entirely so no production file imports `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, or `usePanes()` afterward.

Run the exhaustive context audit using the repo-wide declaration and exported-hook inventories: compute field counts using imported local type expansion, compute external production consumer counts while excluding same-file helper wiring/tests/mocks, classify every provider declaration and recomposed context-access hook (`migrate`, `narrow`, `keep`, or `out-of-scope`), and commit the results to `docs/state_management_context_audit.md` with rationale and any explicit exemptions.

For every non-pane surface that exceeds both audit thresholds under the documented counting rules, migrate or narrow it in this same branch using the smallest viable fix: replace recomposed compatibility hooks with narrower exports where possible, move truly wide mutable state to selector-based access where necessary, and leave below-threshold or local UI token contexts documented rather than redesigned.

Add the permanent automated fat-context declaration guard using the same field-count rule documented in the audit: implement the repo-level check (preferably under `scripts/quality/`), keep its scope limited to new over-threshold `createContext(...)` declarations, wire it into an automated repo command or CI quality path, and document the mechanism plus any allowlist/exemption handling in the audit doc and/or repo docs.

Run end-to-end verification and close the branch: write a short throwaway reproduction script that exercises the original broad panes subscription/problem shape and/or the targeted state transitions, run it to confirm the new store/selectors behave as intended, delete the script afterward, run the most relevant focused tests first, then run `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm test`, grep-verify that no production file imports `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, or `usePanes()`, and if any test or typecheck fails, fix the code and re-run until clean. For deferred-human runtime-only checks such as telemetry-overlay rerender confirmation and small-viewport exclusivity behavior, mark them `skipped` on first encounter with executor notes citing the sandbox/runtime/browser limitation.

watch_items: Panes are already split across `PaneLockPolicyContext`, `PaneLayoutContext`, and `PaneSelectionContext`; the real hotspot is the recomposed `usePanes()` surface plus provider-owned bootstrap state. Judge success by deletion of that broad facade, not by the stale 'single fat context' wording.
Do not recreate a broad panes compatibility API on top of Zustand. High-level panes consumers must move to dedicated selectors or tightly scoped grouped selectors only where genuinely necessary.

Preserve mount order and initialization timing in `src/app/providers/AppProviders.tsx`; panes bootstrap is load-bearing relative to `ProjectProvider`, `RealtimeProvider`, `IncomingTasksProvider`, and `AgentChatProvider`.
Preserve pane-lock policy behavior, including mobile/tablet exclusivity, persisted `paneLocks` hydration, and tasks-pane open/lock coupling from the current bootstrap logic.
Define the panes-store singleton lifecycle contract explicitly. Add an idempotent bootstrap and a reset helper to avoid cross-test leakage; do not leave behavior to incidental module state.
Audit for side effects currently embedded in panes/provider logic before deletion, including any open/lock transitions that may trigger analytics or downstream coordination.

The context audit must start from a repo-wide exported hook inventory, not path-limited guesses. Include surfaces such as `useProject`, media-lightbox safe hooks, travel-between-images hooks, and `useImageGenerationFormContexts`.

Use the documented field-count rule consistently in both the audit doc and the permanent guard: expand imported local repository types recursively until a documented cutoff; third-party or opaque types count as one.
Consumer counts for wrapper hooks must use external production consumers only. Exclude same-file helper composition, tests, and mocks from threshold decisions.
Keep scope tight during the audit: framework/local UI token contexts may be marked `out-of-scope` or `keep` with rationale rather than migrated.
Every audited non-pane surface above both thresholds must be migrated or narrowed in this branch; do not stop after panes if additional over-threshold surfaces are found.
Do not worsen unrelated debt-watch items elsewhere in the repo; ignore unrelated dirty-worktree changes unless they directly conflict with this milestone.

Deferred-human checks are first-class `skipped` outcomes in this sandbox. If browser/runtime/device/manual verification is required, skip immediately with concrete blocker evidence rather than blocking or retrying.

sense_checks.question: Did the inventory capture the full panes caller/test surface, the generic-aware `createContext` declaration set, and the repo-wide exported hook set needed for an exhaustive audit?
Does `src/shared/state/panesStore.ts` now provide selector-first panes state with explicit bootstrap/reset lifecycle semantics and preserved pane behavior?
Were all production panes consumers migrated off `usePanes()` to narrow selectors without recreating a broad grouped panes facade, and is provider-tree mount order preserved?
Are panes tests/mocks updated for the store lifecycle, and are `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, and `usePanes()` fully deleted from production usage?
Does `docs/state_management_context_audit.md` cover every remaining context declaration and recomposed access hook with field count, external production consumer count, decision, and rationale?
For every non-pane surface above both thresholds, was the surface actually migrated or narrowed in this branch rather than merely documented?

Is there now an automated repo check that flags new over-threshold `createContext(...)` declarations using the same counting rule documented in the audit, and is it wired into an automated quality path?

Did the executor run a throwaway reproduction script, delete it afterward, pass `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm test`, and verify by grep that no production file imports `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, or `usePanes()`?

meta_commentary: Execution should follow the concrete hot path, not the stale prose framing. The repository already split panes into three contexts; the performance win comes from deleting `usePanes()` and provider-owned pane state in favor of narrow store selectors and explicit bootstrap state. Treat T1 as the authoritative checklist generator so later work is inventory-backed rather than sample-driven. During the audit, keep the counting contract disciplined: imported local types must be expanded for field counts, while consumer counts must exclude same-file composition/tests/mocks. The permanent guard is intentionally narrower than the audit: it blocks new fat `createContext(...)` declarations only, while recomposed wrapper-hook anti-patterns are handled as milestone migration work, not permanent lint scope. The final verification task must include a disposable reproduction script, focused test passes before full suite runs, and immediate `skipped` handling for browser/runtime/device-only checks with explicit blocker notes.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Build complete repo-wide inventory for panes, contexts, and wrapper hooks
Introduce selector-first panes store with lifecycle contract
Migrate all panes callers to narrow selectors and delete old panes surface
Audit provider declarations and recomposed access hooks with accurate counting rules
Migrate or narrow every over-threshold non-pane surface
Prove the milestone end-to-end with tests, grep checks, and required artifacts

validation.completeness_notes: All seven approved plan steps are covered. The split between T3 and T4 is deliberate: Step 3 in the approved plan spans both production consumer migration and test/mock deletion work, so it maps to two execution tasks for clarity. Deferred-human runtime checks remain intentionally handled inside T8 as `skipped` if encountered because the sandbox cannot produce browser/device evidence.

baseline_test_note: No supported test runner detected on PATH (looked for: pytest). Configure test_command in state config to specify one.
